creator: vedad@kajtaz.net
text:
Hello, I'm hijacking this thread (I'm not the OP).

I'm actively using garage, and also deploying a slightly modified (poudriere + overlay) port on my servers with a few fixes (boot order I mentioned in another PR, building with syslog support, and using 1.0.1 which has a major bugfix). I've also made a tiny FreeBSD-specific contribution to the upstream.

I was (finally) considering submitting my changes when I discovered this thread.

I'd gladly take over the port maintenance, but I've never done so, hence I'd need some pointers on how to proceed. I did take a look at the porter handbook, and also the git mirror (not mentioned in the handbook), so I'm unsure about the best future-proof way of proceeding.

Finally, as mentioned in this thread, garage has an update procedure that disallows skipping intermediate version (eg. one cannot skip 0.9 when upgrading from 0.8 to 1.0), therefore I believe the port itself should be versioned (eg. garage10 for 1.0), so that multiple major versions are available at once, at least starting with 1.0. I guess that'd imply renaming the port, and I need some hints on best practice regarding that as well.

Thanks,
